  • Catalyst Layers

Catalyst Layers#

CatalystLayer extends PorousLayer with an ionomer phase and an electrochemical reaction model. It provides ionomer charge resistance via the Neyerlin/Goshtasbi distribution model and activation overpotential from Butler-Volmer kinetics.

PtCCatalystLayer adds explicit Pt/C agglomerate geometry following Hao et al. (2015): volume fractions are derived from the catalyst composition and the wet ionomer film thickness is updated at each operating point. Local O₂ transport resistance across the ionomer film (bulk + gas/Pt and ionomer/Pt interface terms) is evaluated at each solve step.

The electrochemical reaction is parameterised by ElectrochemicalReaction, which holds the Butler-Volmer parameters and the Nernst correction.

Components#

class marapendi.porous_layers.catalyst_layers.CatalystLayer(thickness=0.001, gas=<factory>, temperature=300.0, pressure=100000.0, porosity=1, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio=1, pore_diameter=1000000000000.0, transport_resistance_model=<factory>, two_phase_transport_model=<factory>, non_wetting_saturation=0, thermal_conductivity=1000000000000.0, absolute_permeability=1000000.0, relative_permeability_exponent=3, contact_angle=95.0, non_wetting_phase='water', wetting_phase='gas', breakthrough_pressure=None, ionomer=<factory>, reaction=<factory>, catalyst_loading=0.002, ionomer_to_catalyst_ratio=0.75, catalyst_density=21450, ecsa=70000.0, ionomer_vol_fraction=0.3, ionomer_film_thickness=2e-09)[source]#

Bases: PorousLayer

Base class for catalyst layers in fuel cells and electrolysers.

Combines a porous electrode structure with an ionomer phase and an electrochemical reaction model. Provides ionomer/electrolyte charge resistance and activation overpotential; subclasses add geometry-specific transport models.

Parameters:

  • thickness (float)

  • gas (GasState)

  • temperature (float)

  • pressure (float)

  • porosity (float)

  • effective_gas_diffusion_ratio (float)

  • pore_diameter (float)

  • transport_resistance_model (PorousGasDiffusionModel)

  • two_phase_transport_model (DarcyTransportModel)

  • non_wetting_saturation (float)

  • thermal_conductivity (float)

  • absolute_permeability (float)

  • relative_permeability_exponent (float)

  • contact_angle (float)

  • non_wetting_phase (str)

  • wetting_phase (str)

  • breakthrough_pressure (float)

  • ionomer (Ionomer)

  • reaction (ElectrochemicalReaction)

  • catalyst_loading (float)

  • ionomer_to_catalyst_ratio (float)

  • catalyst_density (float)

  • ecsa (float)

  • ionomer_vol_fraction (float)

  • ionomer_film_thickness (float)

ionomer#

Ionomer transport model (proton/hydroxide conductivity, O₂ permeability).

Type:

Ionomer

reaction#

Electrochemical reaction parameters (exchange current density, Tafel slope).

Type:

ElectrochemicalReaction

catalyst_loading#

Total catalyst loading (kg/m²).

Type:

float

ionomer_to_catalyst_ratio#

Ionomer/catalyst mass ratio (–).

Type:

float

catalyst_density#

Catalyst particle density (kg/m³).

Type:

float

ecsa#

Electrochemically active surface area (m²/kg).

Type:

float

ionomer_vol_fraction#

Volume fraction of ionomer (–).

Type:

float

ionomer_film_thickness#

Ionomer film thickness around catalyst particles (m).

Type:

float

contact_angle#

Contact angle between liquid water and the solid phase (°).

Type:

float

catalyst_density: float = 21450#
catalyst_loading: float = 0.002#
contact_angle: float = 95.0#
ecsa: float = 70000.0#
effective_charge_resistance(current_density, ionomer_water_content, temperature, charge='proton')[source]#

Calculate effective charge resistance using the equations from Goshtasbi et al. (2020) based on Neyerlin et al. (2007).

Parameters:

  • current_density (float) – Current density [A/m²].

  • ionomer_water_content (float) – Water content in the ionomer.

  • temperature (float) – Temperature in Kelvin.

  • charge (str) – Charge carrier type.

Returns:

Effective charge resistance [Ohm.m²].

Return type:

float

References

Neyerlin, K. C. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 154, B279 (2007). Goshtasbi, A. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 167, 024518 (2020).

electrolyte_sheet_resistance(temperature, electrolyte_saturation=0)[source]#

Calculate electrolyte sheet resistance in the porous electrolyte phase. Uses Bruggeman correlation for electrolyte.

Parameters:

  • temperature (float) – Temperature in Kelvin.

  • electrolyte_saturation (float) – Electrolyte saturation.

Returns:

Electrolyte sheet resistance [Ohm.m²].

Return type:

float

ionomer: Ionomer#
ionomer_film_thickness: float = 2e-09#
ionomer_sheet_charge_resistance(ionomer_water_content, temperature, charge='proton')[source]#

Compute ionomer film proton resistance.

Parameters:

  • ionomer_water_content (float) – Water content.

  • temperature (float) – Temperature in Kelvin.

  • charge (str) – Charge carrier.

Returns:

Ionomer resistance [Ohm.m²].

Return type:

float

References

Hao, L. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 163, F744 (2016).

ionomer_to_catalyst_ratio: float = 0.75#
ionomer_tortuosity(volume_fraction)[source]#

Bruggeman tortuosity factor for the ionomer phase.

Standard Bruggeman inverse power-law approximation for a randomly distributed phase with exponent -0.5.

Parameters:

volume_fraction (float) – Ionomer volume fraction (n.d.).

Returns:

Tortuosity factor (n.d.).

Return type:

float

ionomer_vol_fraction: float = 0.3#
reaction: ElectrochemicalReaction#
class marapendi.porous_layers.catalyst_layers.PtCCatalystLayer(thickness=0.001, gas=<factory>, temperature=300.0, pressure=100000.0, porosity=None, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio=1, pore_diameter=1000000000000.0, transport_resistance_model=<factory>, two_phase_transport_model=<factory>, non_wetting_saturation=0, thermal_conductivity=1000000000000.0, absolute_permeability=1000000.0, relative_permeability_exponent=3, contact_angle=95.0, non_wetting_phase='water', wetting_phase='gas', breakthrough_pressure=None, ionomer=<factory>, reaction=<factory>, catalyst_loading=0.002, ionomer_to_catalyst_ratio=0.75, catalyst_density=21450, ecsa=70000.0, ionomer_vol_fraction=0, ionomer_film_thickness=0, platinum_loading=0.002, catalyst_platinum_weight_percent=0.5, ionomer_to_carbon_ratio=0.75, platinum_density=21450, carbon_density=1950, platinum_vol_surface_area=0, carbon_agglomerate_radius=2.5e-08, dry_ionomer_vol_fraction=0, omega_PtO=3000000.0, ionomer_k1=8.5, ionomer_k2=5.4, ionomer_k3=5.4, ionomer_water_content=10)[source]#

Bases: CatalystLayer

Catalyst layer with explicit Pt/C agglomerate geometry and ionomer film.

Volume fractions (carbon, platinum, ionomer, pore) are derived from the catalyst composition at construction time. The wet ionomer film thickness is updated at each operating point via set_ionomer_wet_properties().

Local O₂ transport resistance across the ionomer film is modelled following Hao et al. (2015): bulk diffusion through the film plus gas/ionomer and ionomer/Pt interface resistances weighted by empirical factors ionomer_k1 (gas-side) and ionomer_k2 (Pt-side). A water-film correction term (ionomer_k3) accounts for flooding at the ionomer surface.

Parameters:

  • thickness (float)

  • gas (GasState)

  • temperature (float)

  • pressure (float)

  • porosity (float)

  • effective_gas_diffusion_ratio (float)

  • pore_diameter (float)

  • transport_resistance_model (PorousGasDiffusionModel)

  • two_phase_transport_model (DarcyTransportModel)

  • non_wetting_saturation (float)

  • thermal_conductivity (float)

  • absolute_permeability (float)

  • relative_permeability_exponent (float)

  • contact_angle (float)

  • non_wetting_phase (str)

  • wetting_phase (str)

  • breakthrough_pressure (float)

  • ionomer (Ionomer)

  • reaction (ElectrochemicalReaction)

  • catalyst_loading (float)

  • ionomer_to_catalyst_ratio (float)

  • catalyst_density (float)

  • ecsa (float)

  • ionomer_vol_fraction (float)

  • ionomer_film_thickness (float)

  • platinum_loading (float)

  • catalyst_platinum_weight_percent (float)

  • ionomer_to_carbon_ratio (float)

  • platinum_density (float)

  • carbon_density (float)

  • platinum_vol_surface_area (float)

  • carbon_agglomerate_radius (float)

  • dry_ionomer_vol_fraction (float)

  • omega_PtO (float)

  • ionomer_k1 (float)

  • ionomer_k2 (float)

  • ionomer_k3 (float)

  • ionomer_water_content (float)

platinum_loading#

Pt loading (kg/m²).

Type:

float

catalyst_platinum_weight_percent#

Pt weight fraction in the catalyst powder (–).

Type:

float

ionomer_to_carbon_ratio#

Ionomer/carbon mass ratio (–).

Type:

float

platinum_density, carbon_density

Material densities (kg/m³).

Type:

float

carbon_agglomerate_radius#

Radius of carbon agglomerates (m).

Type:

float

ionomer_k1, ionomer_k2, ionomer_k3

Interface resistance pre-factors from Hao et al. (2015).

Type:

float

omega_PtO#

Molar volume of platinum oxide (m³/kmol), used by the PtO coverage model.

Type:

float

References

Hao, L. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 162, F854 (2015).

carbon_agglomerate_radius: float = 2.5e-08#
carbon_density: float = 1950#
catalyst_platinum_weight_percent: float = 0.5#
contact_angle: float = 95.0#
dry_ionomer_vol_fraction: float = 0#
ionomer_film_thickness: float = 0#
ionomer_k1: float = 8.5#
ionomer_k2: float = 5.4#
ionomer_k3: float = 5.4#
ionomer_to_carbon_ratio: float = 0.75#
ionomer_tortuosity(volume_fraction)[source]#

Piecewise tortuosity factor for the ionomer film in a Pt/C agglomerate layer.

Above a threshold of 0.16 the ionomer network is well-connected and the tortuosity approaches 1. Below that threshold a fitted power law is used, following the correlation from Hao et al. (2015).

Parameters:

volume_fraction (float) – Ionomer volume fraction (–).

Returns:

Tortuosity factor (–).

Return type:

float

References

Hao, L. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 162, F854 (2015).

ionomer_vol_fraction: float = 0#
ionomer_water_content: float = 10#
o2_ionomer_film_bulk_resistance(ionomer_water_content, temperature)[source]#

Calculate the oxygen bulk resistance across the ionomer film.

Parameters:

  • ionomer_water_content (float) – Water content in the ionomer.

  • temperature (float) – Temperature in Kelvin.

Returns:

Oxygen film resistance [s/m].

Return type:

float

o2_ionomer_film_resistance(ionomer_water_content, temperature, catalyst_availability=1)[source]#

Calculate total oxygen film resistance. Interface resistances are calculated according to equation 32 in Hao et al. (2015), but neglecting the effect of the water film.

Parameters:

  • ionomer_water_content (float) – Water content in ionomer.

  • temperature (float) – Temperature in Kelvin.

  • catalyst_availability (float) – Catalyst availability = 1 - catalyst coverage ratio.

Returns:

Total oxygen film resistance [s/m].

Return type:

float

References

Hao, L. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 162, F854 (2015).

omega_PtO: float = 3000000.0#
platinum_density: float = 21450#
platinum_loading: float = 0.002#
platinum_vol_surface_area: float = 0#
porosity: float = None#
set_ionomer_wet_properties(ionomer_water_content, temperature)[source]#

Update ionomer volume fraction, film thickness, and pore volume for given water content.

Computes the wet ionomer expansion factor from the ionomer model, then recalculates ionomer_vol_fraction, porosity, ionomer_film_thickness, ionomer_vol_surface_area, and effective_gas_diffusion_ratio. Called at construction and at every MEA temperature update.

set_water_film_thickness(water_saturation)[source]#

Compute and store the liquid water film thickness around agglomerates.

Parameters:

water_saturation (float) – Non-wetting (liquid water) saturation in the pore space (–).

Electrochemical reaction#

class marapendi.thermo.electrochemistry.ElectrochemicalReaction(reference_exchange_current_density=0.0741, activation_energy=67000000.0, reaction_order=0.54, reference_activity=100000.0, reference_temperature=353.15, number_of_electrons=2, charge_transfer_coeff=0.5)[source]#

Bases: object

Parameters of a single-reactant electrochemical reaction (ORR or HOR).

Stores the kinetic constants required to evaluate exchange current density and activation overpotential via Butler-Volmer / Tafel expressions.

Parameters:

  • reference_exchange_current_density (float) – Reference exchange current density at reference_temperature and reference_activity in A/m² (per unit geometric area).

  • activation_energy (float) – Arrhenius activation energy in J/kmol.

  • reaction_order (float) – Reaction order with respect to the reactant activity (dimensionless).

  • reference_activity (float) – Reactant activity at the reference state in Pa (or the same units as the activity passed to the kinetic methods).

  • reference_temperature (float) – Temperature at which reference_exchange_current_density is defined, in K.

  • number_of_electrons (int) – Electrons transferred per elementary step.

  • charge_transfer_coeff (float) – Charge-transfer (symmetry) coefficient (dimensionless).

activation_energy: float = 67000000.0#
charge_transfer_coeff: float = 0.5#
exchange_current_density(temperature, reactant_activity)[source]#

Exchange current density at the given temperature and reactant activity.

Parameters:

  • temperature (float) – Temperature in K.

  • reactant_activity (float) – Reactant activity (same units as reference_activity).

Returns:

Exchange current density i₀ in A/m².

Return type:

float

linear_overpotential(current_density, temperature, reactant_activity)[source]#

Linear (low-overpotential) activation overpotential.

Parameters:

  • current_density (float) – Operating current density in A/m².

  • temperature (float) – Temperature in K.

  • reactant_activity (float) – Reactant activity (same units as reference_activity).

Returns:

Linear activation overpotential in V.

Return type:

float

number_of_electrons: int = 2#
reaction_order: float = 0.54#
reference_activity: float = 100000.0#
reference_exchange_current_density: float = 0.0741#
reference_temperature: float = 353.15#
tafel_overpotential(current_density, temperature, reactant_activity)[source]#

Activation overpotential for the given operating conditions.

Parameters:

  • current_density (float) – Operating current density in A/m².

  • temperature (float) – Temperature in K.

  • reactant_activity (float) – Reactant activity (same units as reference_activity).

Returns:

Activation overpotential in V.

Return type:

float

tafel_slope(temperature)[source]#

Tafel slope at the given temperature.

Parameters:

temperature (float) – Temperature in K.

Returns:

Tafel slope in V.

Return type:

float
